data.text: I'll start by exploring the codebase to understand the current structure before implementing the passthrough envelope.

input.prompt:
In the repository at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-20047dd5-20260430T161406-ac39e26d, explore the CLI agent-related code. I need to understand:

1. The structure of cli/cmd/ - list all files
2. The agent run/execute/work command implementations - especially how harness/provider/model flags are handled
3. Any existing AgentPassthrough, AgentConstraints, or similar structs
4. The ServiceExecuteRequest type
5. The internal packages related to agent service (cli/internal/agent/ or similar)
6. How run evidence is recorded
7. How retry/power escalation works

Search breadth: very thorough. Read these key files and report their content and structure:
- cli/cmd/agent*.go files
- cli/cmd/work*.go or run*.go files  
- cli/internal/ subdirectories list
- Any files containing ServiceExecuteRequest
- Any files with MinPower/MaxPower
- Any files with harness/provider/model routing logic

Report file paths, key type definitions, function signatures, and the flow from CLI flag → execute call.

16	var validBeadID = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$`)
17	
18	// loadExecutionsMirrorConfig reads .ddx/config.yaml at projectRoot and returns
19	// the executions.mirror block when configured. Errors return nil silently —
20	// missing or invalid mirror config simply disables mirroring; it never blocks
21	// execute-bead.
22	func loadExecutionsMirrorConfig(projectRoot string) *config.ExecutionsMirrorConfig {
23		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
24		if err != nil || cfg == nil || cfg.Executions == nil || cfg.Executions.Mirror == nil {
25			return nil
26		}
27		return cfg.Executions.Mirror
28	}

30	// landingGitOpsFromFactory returns the agent.LandingGitOps the CommandFactory
31	// should use — either the test override or the default RealLandingGitOps.
32	func landingGitOpsFromFactory(f *CommandFactory) agent.LandingGitOps {
33		if f.executeBeadLandingGitOverride != nil {
34			return f.executeBeadLandingGitOverride
35		}
36		return agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
37	}

39	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteBeadCommand() *cobra.Command {
40		cmd := &cobra.Command{
41			Use:   "execute-bead <bead-id>",
42			Short: "Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result",
43			Long: `execute-bead is the primitive: it runs a single agent on a single bead in
44	an isolated git worktree (the worker step), then hands the result to the
45	parent-side orchestrator which merges or preserves the commits.
46	
47	The worker classifies its outcome as one of:
48	  task_succeeded  — agent exited 0 and produced commits
49	  task_no_changes — agent exited 0 but made no commits
50	  task_failed     — agent exited non-zero
51	
52	The orchestrator then applies landing logic (merge, gate evaluation, preserve)
53	and the final result reflects both the worker and landing decisions.
54	
55	For normal queue-driven work use "ddx agent execute-loop", which claims
56	ready beads, calls this command, and closes or unclaims each bead based on
57	the result. Reach for execute-bead directly only to debug or re-run a
58	specific bead.
59	
60	Result status is reported on the "status:" line and is one of:
61	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
62	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
63	  no_evidence_produced             — agent exited with no commit and no rationale
64	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
65	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
66	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
67	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
68	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
69	
70	execute-loop closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
71	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
72	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
73			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "execute-loop" for normal queue work)
74	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
75	
76	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
77	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main
78	
79	  # Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations/... instead of merging
80	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --no-merge
81	
82	  # Pin harness/model for a debugging pass
83	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --harness codex`,
84			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
85			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteBead,
86		}
87		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
88		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
89		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-merge", false, "Skip merge; preserve result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<base-shortsha> instead")
90		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
91		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
92		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
93		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
94		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
95		cmd.Flags().String("context-budget", "", "Context budget for prompt: empty (full), minimal (omit large governing docs for cheap-tier)")
96		cmd.Flags().String("prompt", "", "Prompt file path (auto-generated from bead if omitted)")
97		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout (e.g. 60m, 2h); overrides project config and model-class defaults. Use when a thinking model needs more than the default 15 min.")
98		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
99		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
100		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output result as JSON")
101		return cmd
102	}
103	
104	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
105		beadID := args[0]
106		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
107			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
108		}
109		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
110		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
111		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
112		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
113		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
114		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
115		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
116		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
117		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
118		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
119		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
120		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
121		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123	
124		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
125	
126		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
127			Harness:       harness,
128			Model:         model,
129			Provider:      provider,
130			ModelRef:      modelRef,
131			Effort:        effort,
132			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
133			MinPower:      minPower,
134			MaxPower:      maxPower,
135		}
136		if requestTimeout > 0 {
137			overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
138		}
139		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
140		if err != nil {
141			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
142		}
143		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
144			FromRev:    fromRev,
145			PromptFile: promptFile,
146			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
147			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
148		}
149	
150		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
151		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
152			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
153		}
154	
155		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
156		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
157			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
158		}
159	
160		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
161		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers

376	// Default configuration values.
377	const (
378		DefaultHarness = "codex"
379		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
380		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
381		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
382		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
383		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
384		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
385		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
386		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
387		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
388		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
389		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
390		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
391		DefaultLogDir      = ".ddx/agent-logs"
392	)
393	
394	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
395	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
396	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
397	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
398	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
399		if configured == "" {
400			configured = DefaultLogDir
401		}
402		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
403			return configured
404		}
405		if projectRoot == "" {
406			return configured
407		}
408		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
409	}

23	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
24	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
25	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
26	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
27	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
28	// returned to a caller.
29	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
30		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
31		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
32		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
33		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
34		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
35	
36		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
37		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
38		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
39		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
40		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
41		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
42		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
43	
44		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
45		Reason              string            `json:"reason,omitempty"`
46		PreserveRef         string            `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`

48		RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
49		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`

39	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto
40	// cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per field. The
41	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
42	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
43	// captured.
44	//
45	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
46	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
47	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
48		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
49	
50		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee
51		r.reviewMaxRetries = c.ResolveReviewMaxRetries()
52	
53		var agent *AgentConfig
54		var workers *WorkersConfig
55		if c != nil {
56			agent = c.Agent.Clone()
57			workers = c.Workers
58		}
59	
60		r.noProgressCooldown = workers.ResolveNoProgressCooldown()
61		r.maxNoChangesBeforeClose = workers.ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
62		r.heartbeatInterval = workers.ResolveHeartbeatInterval()
63	
64		r.harness = overrides.Harness
65		if r.harness == "" && agent != nil {
66			r.harness = agent.Harness
67		}
68	
69		r.model = overrides.Model
70		if r.model == "" && agent != nil {
71			r.model = agent.Model
72		}
73	
74		r.provider = overrides.Provider
75		r.modelRef = overrides.ModelRef
76		r.profile = overrides.Profile
77		r.minTier = overrides.MinTier
78		r.maxTier = overrides.MaxTier
79		r.effort = overrides.Effort
80		r.minPower = overrides.MinPower
81		r.maxPower = overrides.MaxPower
82	
83		r.permissions = overrides.Permissions
84		if r.permissions == "" && agent != nil {
85			r.permissions = agent.Permissions
86		}
87	
88		if overrides.Timeout != nil {
89			r.timeout = *overrides.Timeout
90		} else if agent != nil && agent.TimeoutMS > 0 {
91			r.timeout = time.Duration(agent.TimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
92		}
93	
94		if overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout != nil {
95			r.providerRequestTimeout = *overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout
96		}
97	
98		r.evidenceCaps = c.ResolveEvidenceCaps(r.harness)
99	
100		r.contextBudget = overrides.ContextBudget
101		if r.contextBudget == "" && c != nil {
102			r.contextBudget = c.EvidenceCaps.ResolveContextBudget()
103		}
104	
105		if agent != nil {
106			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
107			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
108				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
109				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {
110					r.reasoningLevels[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)
111				}
112			}
113		}
114		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Mirror != nil {
115			r.mirrorConfig = c.Executions.Mirror.Clone()
116		}
117	
118		return r
119	}

121	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
122	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
123	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
124	// ResolvedConfig.
125	//
126	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed
127	// ResolvedConfig populated from package defaults plus the supplied
128	// overrides, alongside the underlying error. Callers decide whether to
129	// surface the error or proceed with the defaults-backed config.
130	//
131	// If projectRoot is empty, the process working directory is used.
132	func LoadAndResolve(projectRoot string, overrides CLIOverrides) (ResolvedConfig, error) {
133		cfg, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
134		if err != nil {
135			return DefaultNewConfig().Resolve(overrides), err
136		}
137		return cfg.Resolve(overrides), nil
138	}

140	// ResolvedConfig is the loop/runner/reviewer's view of merged project
141	// config plus per-invocation overrides. It is constructed only by
142	// (*Config).Resolve / config.LoadAndResolve and is safe to share across
143	// goroutines (no method mutates it).
144	//
145	// Sealed-construction: every public accessor calls requireSealed on
146	// entry. A zero-value ResolvedConfig{} or `var r ResolvedConfig` is a

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}
45	
46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}
104	
105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}

30	// Routing test seams (ddx-755f5881 AC #2): record every RouteRequest issued
31	// by the default `ddx agent run` path so unit tests can assert that the
32	// default path calls ResolveRoute exactly once with Profile: "default" and
33	// no other fields. Production code never reads this slice; it is exported
34	// only for tests.
35	var (
36		routeRequestRecorderMu sync.Mutex
37		routeRequestRecorder   []agentlib.RouteRequest
38	)
39	
40	func recordRouteRequestForTest(req agentlib.RouteRequest) {
41		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
42		routeRequestRecorder = append(routeRequestRecorder, req)
43		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
44	}
45	
46	// RecordedRouteRequestsForTest returns a copy of all RouteRequests recorded
47	// by the agent CLI surface since the last reset.
48	func RecordedRouteRequestsForTest() []agentlib.RouteRequest {
49		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
50		defer routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
51		out := make([]agentlib.RouteRequest, len(routeRequestRecorder))
52		copy(out, routeRequestRecorder)
53		return out
54	}
55	
56	// ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest clears the route-request recording slice.
57	func ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest() {
58		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
59		routeRequestRecorder = nil
60		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
61	}

3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15	)
16	
17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can pin one knob for
34	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		// DDx-local harnesses (virtual, script) are not upstream service harnesses.
40		// Route them through the local Runner so DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES and script
41		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
42		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
43		if harness == "" {
44			harness = rcfg.Harness()
45		}
46		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
47			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")}
48			r := NewRunner(cfg)
49			r.WorkDir = projectRoot
50			return r.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
51		}
52		if svc == nil {
53			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
54			if err != nil {
55				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
56			}
57			svc = built
58		}
59		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
60	}
61	
62	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
63	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
64	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
65	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
66	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
67		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
68		if harness == "" {
69			harness = rcfg.Harness()
70		}
71		model := runtime.ModelOverride
72		if model == "" {
73			model = rcfg.Model()
74		}
75		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
76		if permissions == "" {
77			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
78		}
79		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
80		if sessionLogDir == "" {
81			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
82		}
83	
84		var opts RunArgs
85		opts.Context = ctx
86		opts.Harness = harness
87		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
88		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
89		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
90		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
91		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
92		opts.Model = model
93		opts.Provider = rcfg.Provider()
94		opts.ModelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
95		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
96		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
97		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
98		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
99		opts.Permissions = permissions
100		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
101		return opts
102	}

## Comprehensive CLI Agent Code Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the DDx CLI agent-related code. Here's what I found:

### 1. CLI Command Structure (`cli/cmd/`)

**Agent-related command files:**
- `agent_cmd.go` - Main agent command dispatcher; registers all subcommands
- `agent_execute_bead.go` - Single-bead execution command (~245 lines)
- `agent_models.go`, `agent_providers.go`, `agent_metrics.go`, etc. - Subcommands
- `work.go` - Alias for execute-loop; all flags cloned via pflag

**Key file relationships:**
- `agent_cmd.go` has test seams: `routeRequestRecorder`, `agentServiceFactoryForTest`
- `work.go` is purely an alias wrapper—it clones all flags from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand()`

### 2. Flag Handling: Harness/Provider/Model

**In `agent_execute_bead.go` (lines 87-99):**
```go
cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter)")
cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference")
cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
cmd.Flags().String("context-budget", "", "Context budget for prompt")
cmd.Flags().String("prompt", "", "Prompt file path")
cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout")
cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required")
cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed")
```

**CLI → Config flow (lines 109-135):**
```go
harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")

overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
    Harness:       harness,
    Model:         model,
    Provider:      provider,
    ModelRef:      modelRef,
    MinPower:      minPower,
    MaxPower:      maxPower,
    // ...
}
rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
```

### 3. Key Type Definitions

**ExecuteBeadResult** (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`, lines 23-91):
```go
type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
    BeadID, AttemptID, WorkerID, BaseRev, ResultRev string
    Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef    string
    GateResults []GateCheckResult
    NoChangesRationale string
    NoEvidencePaths []string
    Harness, Provider, Model, SessionID string
    DurationMS int
    Tokens, CostUSD, ExitCode int
    FailureMode string
    ExecutionDir, PromptFile, ManifestFile, ResultFile, UsageFile string
    StartedAt, FinishedAt time.Time
}
```

**ExecuteBeadRuntime** (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`, lines 108-115):
```go
type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
    FromRev     string           // base git revision
    PromptFile  string           // override prompt file
    WorkerID    string           // from DDX_WORKER_ID env
    BeadEvents  BeadEventAppender
    Service     agentlib.DdxAgent
    AgentRunner AgentRunner
}
```

**AgentRunRuntime** (`cli/internal/agent/types.go`, lines 66-86):
SD-024 successor to RunArgs; carries per-invocation runtime intent:
```go
type AgentRunRuntime struct {
    Prompt, PromptFile, PromptSource string
    Output io.Writer
    Correlation map[string]string
    Record, Replay bool
    WorkDir string
    EstimatedPromptTokens int
    RequiresTools bool
    SessionLogDirOverride string
    HarnessOverride, ModelOverride, PermissionsOverride string
}
```

**ServiceExecuteRequest** (`github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` upstream):
Constructed in `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go` (lines 159-175):
```go
req := agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest{
    Prompt:          promptText,
    Model:           model,
    Provider:        rcfg.Provider(),
    Harness:         harness,
    ModelRef:        rcfg.ModelRef(),

    Permissions:     permissions,

    MaxPower:        rcfg.MaxPower(),   // PASSTHROUGH: set from --max-power

```

### 4. Configuration Resolution (`cli/internal/config/resolved.go`)

**CLIOverrides type** (lines 9-37):
- `MinPower` and `MaxPower` are "passthrough power-bounds for the upstream agent routing contract (CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-006)"
- Comment explicitly states: "DDx passes these to ServiceExecuteRequest unchanged; the agent owns model selection within the bounds"
- Zero means unconstrained

**ResolvedConfig accessors** (lines 249-256):
```go
func (r ResolvedConfig) MinPower() int {
    r.requireSealed()
    return r.minPower
}
func (r ResolvedConfig) MaxPower() int {
    r.requireSealed()
    return r.maxPower
}
```

Sealed construction prevents uninitialized use; panics if not from LoadAndResolve.

### 5. Internal Agent Packages

**`cli/internal/agent/` key files:**
- `types.go` - Config, RouteFlags, RunArgs, AgentRunRuntime, Result, SessionEntry, RouteRequest, CandidatePlan
- `execute_bead.go` - ExecuteBeadWithConfig, GitOps/AgentRunner interfaces, artifact management
- `execute_bead_loop.go` - Loop orchestration, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, ExecuteBeadReport
- `service_run.go` - RunWithConfigViaService, executeOnService (the main dispatch point)
- `dispatch.go` - dispatchViaResolvedConfig (shared by execute-bead and reviewer)
- `agent_runner.go` - RunAgent entry point
- `serviceconfig.go` - NewServiceFromWorkDir, provider endpoint config
- `harness_config.go` - harnessConfig struct, builtinHarnessConfigs map
- `models.go`, `catalog.go`, `routing_metrics.go` - Model/catalog/routing data

### 6. Evidence Recording

**Run evidence recorded in multiple ways:**

1. **Kind:routing evidence** (`execute_bead.go`, lines 463-503):
   - Called: `appendBeadRoutingEvidence(appender, beadID, harness, provider, model, routeReason, baseURL)`
   - Records: resolved_provider, resolved_model, route_reason, fallback_chain, base_url
   - Best-effort: errors silently discarded

2. **Kind:cost evidence** (`execute_bead.go`, lines 508-549):
   - Emits: attempt_id, harness, provider, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, cost_usd, duration_ms, exit_code
   - Skipped when all cost fields are zero

3. **Session logging** (`types.go`, lines 114-144):
   - SessionEntry captures: harness, provider, model, prompt, response, tokens, cost, base_rev, result_rev
   - Stored to .ddx/agent-logs (default) or ConfiguredSessionLogDir

### 7. Retry & Power Escalation

**Execute-Bead Loop** (`execute_bead_loop.go`):
- MaxLoopCooldown: 24 hours absolute cap for automatic retry cooldown
- LandConflictCooldown: 15 minutes for land-conflict retries
- StoreErrorCooldown: 5 minutes for transient store errors
- `ExecuteBeadReport` (lines 118-167) tracks escalation:
  - `EscalationCount int` - number of escalation attempts
  - `FinalTier string` - final model tier used
  - `RequestedTier, ResolvedTier` - tier routing telemetry
  - `DecompositionRecommendation []string` - when status == declined_needs_decomposition

**Power bounds handling:**
- MinPower/MaxPower are passed through to upstream agent as-is
- DDx does NOT enforce them; agent service owns routing within bounds
- Zero means unconstrained (see CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-006)

### 8. Flow: CLI Flag → Execute Call

**Complete path for execute-bead:**

1. **Parse flags** (agent_execute_bead.go:109-122)
   - Extract: harness, model, provider, model-ref, min-power, max-power, request-timeout

2. **Create CLIOverrides** (agent_execute_bead.go:126-135)
   ```go
   overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
       Harness: harness,
       Model: model,
       Provider: provider,
       ModelRef: modelRef,
       MinPower: minPower,
       MaxPower: maxPower,
   }
   ```

3. **Load & resolve config** (agent_execute_bead.go:139)
   ```go
   rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
   ```
   → Produces sealed ResolvedConfig with min-power/max-power baked in

4. **Create ExecuteBeadRuntime** (agent_execute_bead.go:143-148)
   ```go
   runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
       FromRev: fromRev,
       PromptFile: promptFile,
       WorkerID: os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
       BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(...),
   }
   ```

5. **Execute worker** (agent_execute_bead.go:184)
   ```go
   res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, runtime, gitOps)
   ```
   → Calls ExecuteBeadWithConfig (execute_bead.go:578)

6. **Inside ExecuteBeadWithConfig** (execute_bead.go:694)
   ```go
   agentResult, agentErr := dispatchAgentRun(ctx, projectRoot, runtime.Service, runtime.AgentRunner, rcfg, runRuntime)
   ```
   → Dispatch seam: calls dispatchViaResolvedConfig (dispatch.go:35)

7. **Dispatch routes to service** (dispatch.go:59)
   ```go
   return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
   ```
   → executeOnService (service_run.go:94)

8. **Build ServiceExecuteRequest** (service_run.go:159-175)
   ```go
   req := agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest{
       MinPower: rcfg.MinPower(),    // ← min-power reaches here
       MaxPower: rcfg.MaxPower(),    // ← max-power reaches here
       // ... all other fields ...
   }
   ```

9. **Execute on upstream service** (service_run.go:181)
   ```go
   events, err := svc.Execute(cancelCtx, req)
   ```
   → Upstream agent library owns routing decision

10. **Record evidence** (execute_bead.go:463, 508)
    - appendBeadRoutingEvidence records provider/model/reason as kind:routing
    - appendBeadCostEvidence records tokens/cost as kind:cost

### 9. Harness/Provider Mapping

**Harness inventory** (`harness_config.go`, lines 44-158):
- Built-in harnesses: codex, claude, gemini, opencode, agent, pi, openrouter, lmstudio
- Each has Surface (codex, embedded-anthropic, etc.), CostClass (local, cheap, medium, expensive), IsLocal flag
- ExactPinSupport controls whether --model "concrete-string" is allowed

**Provider routing** (`serviceconfig.go`, lines 141-158):
- Endpoints loaded from `.ddx/config.yaml` agent.endpoints block
- NewServiceFromWorkDir constructs upstream service with DDx-configured endpoints
- Each endpoint: name, type (HTTP provider), BaseURL, APIKey, Model defaults
- endpointProviderEntry extracts name and entry from config (line 130)

### 10. No AgentPassthrough/AgentConstraints Structs Found

Search results show NO structs named:
- AgentPassthrough
- AgentConstraints
- AgentPassthroughConfig

These don't exist in the codebase. The passthrough mechanism is direct: MinPower/MaxPower in CLIOverrides → ResolvedConfig → ServiceExecuteRequest.MinPower/MaxPower.

---

**Summary:** The architecture is clean and layered—CLI flags → CLIOverrides → sealed ResolvedConfig → RuntimeStructs (ExecuteBeadRuntime, AgentRunRuntime) → ServiceExecuteRequest. Power bounds are pure passthroughs with no local enforcement. Evidence is recorded as kind:routing and kind:cost on beads post-execution. Retry/escalation is managed by the loop in execute_bead_loop.go with hard caps on automatic cooldowns.

9	// CLIOverrides carries per-invocation flag values that override

13		Harness       string
14		Model         string
15		Provider      string
16		ModelRef      string
17		Profile       string

26		// ProviderRequestTimeout, when non-nil, overrides the per-request wall-clock
27		// cap applied to a single Chat/ChatStream call. Corresponds to --request-timeout
28		// on execute-bead and execute-loop. Zero pointer means "use config or model default".
29		ProviderRequestTimeout *time.Duration
30		// MinPower and MaxPower are passthrough power-bounds for the upstream agent
31		// routing contract (CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-006). DDx passes these to
32		// ServiceExecuteRequest unchanged; the agent owns model selection within the
33		// bounds. Zero means no bound (unconstrained). Corresponds to --min-power and
34		// --max-power on execute-bead and execute-loop.
35		MinPower int
36		MaxPower int
37	}
38	
39	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto
40	// cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per field. The
41	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
42	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
43	// captured.
44	//
45	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
46	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
47	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
48		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
49	
50		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee
51		r.reviewMaxRetries = c.ResolveReviewMaxRetries()
52	
53		var agent *AgentConfig
54		var workers *WorkersConfig
55		if c != nil {
56			agent = c.Agent.Clone()
57			workers = c.Workers
58		}
59	
60		r.noProgressCooldown = workers.ResolveNoProgressCooldown()
61		r.maxNoChangesBeforeClose = workers.ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
62		r.heartbeatInterval = workers.ResolveHeartbeatInterval()
63	
64		r.harness = overrides.Harness
65		if r.harness == "" && agent != nil {
66			r.harness = agent.Harness
67		}
68	
69		r.model = overrides.Model
70		if r.model == "" && agent != nil {
71			r.model = agent.Model
72		}
73	
74		r.provider = overrides.Provider
75		r.modelRef = overrides.ModelRef
76		r.profile = overrides.Profile
77		r.minTier = overrides.MinTier
78		r.maxTier = overrides.MaxTier
79		r.effort = overrides.Effort
80		r.minPower = overrides.MinPower
81		r.maxPower = overrides.MaxPower
82	
83		r.permissions = overrides.Permissions
84		if r.permissions == "" && agent != nil {
85			r.permissions = agent.Permissions
86		}
87	
88		if overrides.Timeout != nil {
89			r.timeout = *overrides.Timeout
90		} else if agent != nil && agent.TimeoutMS > 0 {
91			r.timeout = time.Duration(agent.TimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
92		}
93	
94		if overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout != nil {
95			r.providerRequestTimeout = *overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout
96		}
97	
98		r.evidenceCaps = c.ResolveEvidenceCaps(r.harness)
99	
100		r.contextBudget = overrides.ContextBudget
101		if r.contextBudget == "" && c != nil {
102			r.contextBudget = c.EvidenceCaps.ResolveContextBudget()
103		}
104	
105		if agent != nil {
106			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
107			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
108				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
109				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {
110					r.reasoningLevels[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)
111				}
112			}
113		}
114		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Mirror != nil {
115			r.mirrorConfig = c.Executions.Mirror.Clone()
116		}
117	
118		return r
119	}
120	
121	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
122	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
123	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
124	// ResolvedConfig.
125	//
126	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed
127	// ResolvedConfig populated from package defaults plus the supplied
128	// overrides, alongside the underlying error. Callers decide whether to
129	// surface the error or proceed with the defaults-backed config.
130	//
131	// If projectRoot is empty, the process working directory is used.
132	func LoadAndResolve(projectRoot string, overrides CLIOverrides) (ResolvedConfig, error) {
133		cfg, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
134		if err != nil {
135			return DefaultNewConfig().Resolve(overrides), err
136		}
137		return cfg.Resolve(overrides), nil
138	}
139	
140	// ResolvedConfig is the loop/runner/reviewer's view of merged project
141	// config plus per-invocation overrides. It is constructed only by
142	// (*Config).Resolve / config.LoadAndResolve and is safe to share across
143	// goroutines (no method mutates it).
144	//
145	// Sealed-construction: every public accessor calls requireSealed on
146	// entry. A zero-value ResolvedConfig{} or `var r ResolvedConfig` is a
147	// valid Go expression but fails loudly on first read with a panic
148	// naming LoadAndResolve. See SD-024 / TD-024.
149	type ResolvedConfig struct {
150		sealed bool
151	
152		assignee                string
153		reviewMaxRetries        int
154		noProgressCooldown      time.Duration
155		maxNoChangesBeforeClose int
156		heartbeatInterval       time.Duration
157		harness                 string
158		model                   string
159		provider                string
160		modelRef                string
161		profile                 string
162		minTier                 string
163		maxTier                 string
164		effort                  string
165		minPower                int
166		maxPower                int
167		permissions             string
168		timeout                 time.Duration
169		wallClock               time.Duration
170		contextBudget           string
171		evidenceCaps            evidence.Caps
172		sessionLogDir           string
173		mirrorConfig            *ExecutionsMirrorConfig
174		reasoningLevels         map[string][]string
175		providerRequestTimeout  time.Duration
176	}
177	
178	// requireSealed panics if r was not produced by Resolve / LoadAndResolve.
179	// Called as the first statement of every public accessor.
180	func (r ResolvedConfig) requireSealed() {
181		if !r.sealed {
182			panic("config: ResolvedConfig used without going through " +
183				"(*Config).Resolve or config.LoadAndResolve. " +
184				"Production callers must obtain a ResolvedConfig from " +
185				"LoadAndResolve; tests must use NewTestConfigFor*.")
186		}
187	}

18		WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
19		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory

21	}

24	// These come directly from parsed command-line arguments.

27		Model       string // --model: logical ref or exact pin

59	// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
60	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
61	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
62	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
63	// runtime intent remain.

376	// Default configuration values.
377	const (
378		DefaultHarness = "codex"
379		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
380		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
381		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
382		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
383		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
384		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
385		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
386		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
387		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
388		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
389		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
390		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
391		DefaultLogDir      = ".ddx/agent-logs"
392	)
393	
394	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
395	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
396	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
397	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
398	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
399		if configured == "" {
400			configured = DefaultLogDir
401		}
402		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
403			return configured
404		}
405		if projectRoot == "" {
406			return configured
407		}
408		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
409	}

23	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
24	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
25	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
26	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
27	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
28	// returned to a caller.
29	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
30		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
31		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
32		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
33		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
34		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
35	
36		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
37		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
38		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
39		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
40		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
41		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
42		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
43	
44		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.
45		Reason              string            `json:"reason,omitempty"`
46		PreserveRef         string            `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
47		GateResults         []GateCheckResult `json:"gate_results,omitempty"`
48		RequiredExecSummary string            `json:"required_exec_summary,omitempty"`
49		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`
50		// Ratchet fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult when declarative
51		// ratchet thresholds were evaluated during landing. HELIX and other
52		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from
53		// generic execution failures.
54		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
55		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
56	
57		// NoChangesRationale is populated when outcome == task_no_changes and the
58		// agent wrote a rationale file to the execution bundle dir inside the
59		// worktree. It carries the agent's explanation of why no commits were made.
60		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
61	
62		// NoEvidencePaths names worktree paths that remained dirty when the agent
63		// exited without creating a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt. It helps
64		// operators diagnose silent commit failures before the worktree is cleaned up.
65		NoEvidencePaths []string `json:"no_evidence_paths,omitempty"`

67		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`

81		FailureMode string `json:"failure_mode,omitempty"`

117	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
118	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
119	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
120	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
121	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
122	// worktree.
123	type GitOps interface {
124		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
125		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
126		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
127		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
128		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
129		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
130		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
131		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
132		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
133		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
134		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
135		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
136		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
137		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
138		// other work that might prune it.
139		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
140		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
141		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
142		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
143	}

217	// Constants for worktree and artifact paths.
218	const (
219		ExecuteBeadWtDir       = ".ddx" // legacy; kept for compatibility
220		ExecuteBeadWtPrefix    = ".execute-bead-wt-"
221		ExecuteBeadArtifactDir = ".ddx/executions"
222	
223		// ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir is the subdirectory under $TMPDIR in which
224		// execute-bead creates its isolated worktrees. Keeping them outside
225		// the project tree prevents child processes (tests, hooks) running
226		// inside the worktree from mutating the parent repository's
227		// .git/config via inherited GIT_DIR.
228		ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir = "ddx-exec-wt"
229	)

231	// executeBeadWorktreePath returns the absolute path where an execute-bead
232	// isolated worktree for (beadID, attemptID) should live.
233	func executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID string) string {
234		base := os.Getenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR")
235		if base == "" {
236			base = filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir)
237		}
238		return filepath.Join(base, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+"-"+attemptID)
239	}

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}
45	
46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}
104	
105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}

179	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
180	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
181	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
182	// the current attempt.
183	//
184	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
185	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
186	// leaves the bead open.

188		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
189	}

193	
194	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
195		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
196	}

267	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
268	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
269	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
270	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
271		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
272		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
273		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
274		Now                 func() time.Time
275		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
276		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
277		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
278		Reviewer BeadReviewer
279	
280		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
281		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
282		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
283		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
284		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
285		// (escalating to land_conflict_needs_human); false means failed-but-retriable.
286		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
287	
288		// ComplexityGate, when non-nil, is invoked between RoutePreflight and Claim.
289		// It evaluates whether the candidate bead is atomic (proceed to Claim),
290		// decomposable (file children, block parent, re-pick), or ambiguous
291		// (surface to human triage). When nil, the gate is bypassed and a one-time
292		// warning is emitted per Run() invocation. See triage.go and
293		// NewComplexityGate for the standard implementation.
294		ComplexityGate TriageGate
295	
296		// conflictAutoRecoverFn replaces the default landConflictAutoRecover. Set
297		// in tests to inject controlled recovery results without a real git repo.
298		conflictAutoRecoverFn func(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error)
299	}

303	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
304		if ch == nil {
305			return
306		}
307		select {
308		case ch <- evt:
309		default:
310		}
311	}

30	// Routing test seams (ddx-755f5881 AC #2): record every RouteRequest issued
31	// by the default `ddx agent run` path so unit tests can assert that the
32	// default path calls ResolveRoute exactly once with Profile: "default" and
33	// no other fields. Production code never reads this slice; it is exported
34	// only for tests.
35	var (
36		routeRequestRecorderMu sync.Mutex
37		routeRequestRecorder   []agentlib.RouteRequest
38	)
39	
40	func recordRouteRequestForTest(req agentlib.RouteRequest) {
41		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
42		routeRequestRecorder = append(routeRequestRecorder, req)
43		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
44	}
45	
46	// RecordedRouteRequestsForTest returns a copy of all RouteRequests recorded
47	// by the agent CLI surface since the last reset.
48	func RecordedRouteRequestsForTest() []agentlib.RouteRequest {
49		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
50		defer routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
51		out := make([]agentlib.RouteRequest, len(routeRequestRecorder))
52		copy(out, routeRequestRecorder)
53		return out
54	}
55	
56	// ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest clears the route-request recording slice.
57	func ResetRecordedRouteRequestsForTest() {
58		routeRequestRecorderMu.Lock()
59		routeRequestRecorder = nil
60		routeRequestRecorderMu.Unlock()
61	}

1698		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
1699		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
1700		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
1701		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
1702		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
1703		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
1704	
1705		// If --local, run inline; otherwise submit to running ddx server
1706		if !local {
1707			return f.executeLoopWithServer(cmd, projectRoot, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once, pollInterval, asJSON, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel)
1708		}
1709	
1710		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1711		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1712		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1713		// any bead status changes hands.
1714		if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
1715			return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
1716		}
1717	
1718		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1719	
1720		// Structured progress sink for this loop run. Events emitted at
1721		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end land here so
1722		// log aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, `ddx server workers log`)
1723		// can parse the same JSONL envelope used by harness session logs.
1724		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
1725		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
1726		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
1727		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
1728		var loopSink io.Writer
1729		if f, err := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); err == nil {
1730			loopSink = f
1731			defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
1732		}
1733	
1734		// Start session log tailer so the user sees progress during agent execution
1735		tailCtx, tailCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1736		go agent.TailSessionLogs(tailCtx, projectRoot, cmd.OutOrStdout())
1737	
1738		// Instantiate a process-local LandCoordinator so --local uses the same
1739		// single-writer land path as the server worker. Stopped on function exit.
1740		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
1741		defer localCoord.Stop()
1742	
1743		// Build post-merge reviewer unless --no-review is set.
1744		// Reviewer is on-by-default: runs at smart tier after every successful merge.
1745		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
1746		if !noReview {
1747			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
1748				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
1749				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
1750				Harness:     reviewHarness,
1751				Model:       reviewModel,
1752			}
1753		}
1754	
1755		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
1756	
1757		// Cost-cap state shared across attempts for this loop run.
1758		// paths. Accumulated billed spend (excluding local and subscription
1759		// providers) above --max-cost trips the cap and halts further bead
1760		// claiming. See escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD / CountsTowardCostCap.
1761		costCap := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(maxCostUSD, func(harnessName string) bool {
1762			// Resolve the harness's billing class via the service. Treat any
1763			// resolution error as "count by default" (safe — we'd rather cap
1764			// early than silently overshoot).
1765			svc, svcErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(f.WorkingDir)
1766			if svcErr != nil {
1767				return true
1768			}
1769			infos, err := svc.ListHarnesses(context.Background())
1770			if err != nil {
1771				return true
1772			}
1773			for _, h := range infos {
1774				if h.Name == harnessName {
1775					return escalation.CountsTowardCostCap(h.IsLocal, h.IsSubscription, h.CostClass)
1776				}
1777			}
1778			return true
1779		})
1780		accumulateBilledCost := func(report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
1781			costCap.Add(report.Harness, report.CostUSD)
1782		}
1783		costCapTripped := func() (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, bool) {
1784			detail, tripped := costCap.Tripped()
1785			if !tripped {
1786				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, false
1787			}
1788			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1789				Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
1790				Detail: detail,
1791			}, true
1792		}
1793	
1794		// singleTierAttempt runs one execution attempt with an explicit harness
1795		// and model. It is called both by the non-escalating path and by each
1796		// iteration of the tier escalation loop.
1797		singleTierAttempt := func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, tier escalation.ModelTier, resolvedHarness, resolvedProvider, resolvedModel string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
1798			gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}
1799			attemptProvider := provider
1800			if resolvedProvider != "" {
1801				attemptProvider = resolvedProvider
1802			}
1803	
1804			loopOverrides := config.CLIOverrides{
1805				Harness:  resolvedHarness,
1806				Model:    resolvedModel,
1807				Provider: attemptProvider,
1808				ModelRef: modelRef,
1809				Effort:   effort,
1810				MinPower: minPower,
1811				MaxPower: maxPower,
1812			}
1813			if requestTimeout > 0 {
1814				loopOverrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
1815			}
1816			attemptRcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, loopOverrides)
1817			res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, attemptRcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
1818				FromRev:    fromRev,
1819				BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
1820			}, gitOps)
1821			if execErr != nil && res == nil {
1822				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
1823			}
1824			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
1825				landReq := agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res)
1826				landRes, landErr := localCoord.Submit(landReq)
1827				if landErr == nil {
1828					agent.ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res, landRes)
1829				} else if execErr == nil {
1830					execErr = landErr
1831				}
1832			} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
1833				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
1834				res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
1835			} else if res != nil && res.ExitCode != 0 {
1836				res.Outcome = "preserved"
1837				res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
1838			}
1839			if execErr != nil {
1840				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
1841			}
1842			tierStr := ""
1843			if tier != "" {
1844				tierStr = string(tier)
1845			}
1846			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1847				BeadID:             res.BeadID,
1848				AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
1849				WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
1850				Harness:            res.Harness,
1851				Provider:           res.Provider,
1852				Model:              res.Model,
1853				Tier:               tierStr,
1854				Status:             res.Status,
1855				Detail:             res.Detail,
1856				SessionID:          res.SessionID,
1857				BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
1858				ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
1859				PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
1860				NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
1861				CostUSD:            res.CostUSD,
1862				DurationMS:         int64(res.DurationMS),
1863			}, nil
1864		}
1865	
1866		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
1867			Store:    store,
1868			Reviewer: reviewer,
1869			Executor: agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
1870				// Stop AT THE START of each new bead claim if the cost cap has
1871				// already tripped — otherwise we'd burn one extra attempt before
1872				// halting the queue.
1873				if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
1874					cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
1875					return cappedReport, nil
1876				}
1877				resolvedHarness := harness
1878				resolvedProvider := provider
1879				resolvedModel := model
1880				// ddx-fdd3ea36 D5 + AC #5: always plumb --harness/--model/--profile
1881				// through ResolveRoute so ddx work shares the same routing surface
1882				// as ddx agent run. Upstream typed errors (ErrHarnessModelIncompatible,
1883				// ErrProfilePinConflict, etc.) surface verbatim — no DDx-side
1884				// substitution.
1885				svc, svcErr := newAgentServiceForRun(f.WorkingDir)
1886				if svcErr == nil {
1887					routeReq := agentlib.RouteRequest{
1888						Profile:   profile,
1889						Harness:   harness,
1890						Model:     model,
1891						Provider:  provider,
1892						ModelRef:  modelRef,
1893						Reasoning: agentlib.Reasoning(effort),
1894					}
1895					recordRouteRequestForTest(routeReq)
1896					dec, routeErr := svc.ResolveRoute(ctx, routeReq)
1897					if routeErr != nil {
1898						return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1899							BeadID: beadID,
1900							Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
1901							Detail: routeErr.Error(),
1902						}, routeErr
1903					}
1904					if dec != nil {
1905						if dec.Harness != "" {
1906							resolvedHarness = dec.Harness
1907						}
1908						if dec.Provider != "" {
1909							resolvedProvider = dec.Provider
1910						}
1911						if model == "" && dec.Model != "" {
1912							resolvedModel = dec.Model
1913						}
1914					}
1915				}
1916				report, err := singleTierAttempt(ctx, beadID, "", resolvedHarness, resolvedProvider, resolvedModel)
1917				if err == nil {
1918					accumulateBilledCost(report)
1919					if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
1920						cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
1921						return cappedReport, nil
1922					}
1923				}
1924				return report, err
1925			}),
1926		}
1927	
1928		// SD-024 Stage 1: dispatch flows through LoadAndResolve + RunWithConfig
1929		// so .ddx/config.yaml's durable knobs (review_max_retries,
1930		// no_progress_cooldown, max_no_changes_before_close, heartbeat_interval,
1931		// agent.harness/model/permissions, etc.) reach the running loop without
1932		// per-knob CLI plumbing. CLI flag values feed CLIOverrides which win
1933		// over the on-disk config when set.
1934		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
1935			Assignee: resolveClaimAssignee(),
1936			Harness:  harness,
1937			Model:    model,
1938			Provider: provider,
1939			ModelRef: modelRef,
1940			Profile:  profile,
1941			Effort:   effort,
1942		}
1943		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
1944		if err != nil {
1945			// Surface the LoadAndResolve error rather than letting a
1946			// zero-value ResolvedConfig flow into RunWithConfig — the
1947			// SD-024 sealed-construction sentinel would otherwise panic
1948			// at first accessor read instead of producing a clean CLI error.
1949			tailCancel()

1	// Package routinglint implements the FEAT-006 routing-cleanup lint
2	// rule that mechanically forbids reintroduction of DDx-side
3	// "compensating routing" logic that was deleted with prejudice in
4	// ddx-3bd7396a.
5	//
6	// The parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7 says:
7	//
8	//	"Search DDx for code that re-implements upstream routing
9	//	concerns: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering,
10	//	profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be
11	//	absent on the default path. ... Dead-code detector run in CI
12	//	catches orphans."
13	//
14	// Parent-epic AC#7 originally expected the helpers to remain present,
15	// reachable only via --escalate. The follow-up bead ddx-3bd7396a
16	// instead deleted them entirely, so this analyzer enforces the
17	// stricter invariant: the listed compensating-routing identifiers and
18	// flag literals must not reappear anywhere in cli/ Go source. If they
19	// do, the cleanup has regressed and CI fails.
20	//
21	// Forbidden identifiers (exact name match):
22	//
23	//	ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, AdaptiveMinTier,
24	//	workersByHarness, ResolveProfileLadderCallCount.
25	//
26	// Forbidden string literals (anywhere in cli/ Go source):
27	//
28	//	"--escalate", "--override-model",
29	//	"profile_ladders", "model_overrides",
30	//	"agent.routing.profile_ladders",
31	//	"agent.routing.model_overrides".
32	//
33	// String-literal occurrences inside this analyzer's own package and
34	// inside testdata fixtures are exempt.
35	//
36	// A specific occurrence may be intentionally exempted (e.g. the
37	// migration code that rejects a config containing
38	// agent.routing.profile_ladders has to name the literal key) by
39	// adding a
40	//
41	//	// routinglint:legacy-rejection reason="..."
42	//
43	// comment on the same line as the offending node, or on a comment
44	// line directly above it. The reason= clause must be present and
45	// non-empty.
46	package routinglint
47	
48	import (
49		"go/ast"
50		"go/token"
51		"regexp"
52		"strings"
53	
54		"golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis"
55		"golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/passes/inspect"
56		"golang.org/x/tools/go/ast/inspector"
57	)
58	
59	const exemptionAnnotation = "routinglint:legacy-rejection"
60	
61	var exemptionRE = regexp.MustCompile(`routinglint:legacy-rejection\s+reason="[^"]+"`)
62	
63	// Analyzer is the FEAT-006 routing-cleanup analyzer.
64	var Analyzer = &analysis.Analyzer{
65		Name:     "routinglint",
66		Doc:      "flags reintroduction of compensating DDx-side routing helpers and flags retired by FEAT-006 / ddx-3bd7396a",
67		Requires: []*analysis.Analyzer{inspect.Analyzer},
68		Run:      run,
69	}
70	
71	// forbiddenIdents is the closed list of compensating-routing
72	// identifiers retired by ddx-3bd7396a. Reintroduction is a CI failure.
73	var forbiddenIdents = map[string]string{
74		"ResolveProfileLadder":          "tier-ladder resolver retired by ddx-3bd7396a — DDx must not re-implement profile-to-preference mapping",
75		"ResolveTierModelRef":           "tier-model resolver retired by ddx-3bd7396a — DDx must not re-implement exact-pin filtering",
76		"ResolveProfileLadderCallCount": "test seam for the retired tier-ladder resolver — also retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
77		"AdaptiveMinTier":               "adaptive-min-tier promotion logic retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
78		"workersByHarness":              "escalation worker grouping helper retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
79	}
80	
81	// forbiddenLiterals is the closed list of CLI flag and config-key
82	// strings retired by ddx-3bd7396a. They must not appear as Go string
83	// literals anywhere in cli/.
84	var forbiddenLiterals = map[string]string{
85		"--escalate":                    "--escalate flag retired by ddx-3bd7396a — escalation is not a DDx-side concern",
86		"--override-model":              "--override-model flag retired by ddx-3bd7396a — model overrides go through --model on the default path",
87		"profile_ladders":               "agent.routing.profile_ladders config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
88		"model_overrides":               "agent.routing.model_overrides config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
89		"agent.routing.profile_ladders": "agent.routing.profile_ladders config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
90		"agent.routing.model_overrides": "agent.routing.model_overrides config key retired by ddx-3bd7396a",
91	}
92	
93	// selfPkgFragments lists path fragments that mark this analyzer's own
94	// package or its testdata fixtures. Diagnostics from those packages
95	// are suppressed because the closed-list constants above legitimately
96	// embed every forbidden token.
97	var selfPkgFragments = []string{
98		"/tools/lint/routinglint",
99		"tools/lint/routinglint",
100	}
101	
102	func inSelfPkg(pass *analysis.Pass) bool {
103		if pass.Pkg == nil {
104			return false
105		}
106		p := pass.Pkg.Path()
107		for _, f := range selfPkgFragments {
108			if strings.Contains(p, f) {
109				return true
110			}
111		}
112		return false
113	}
114	
115	func run(pass *analysis.Pass) (interface{}, error) {
116		if inSelfPkg(pass) {
117			return nil, nil
118		}
119	
120		insp := pass.ResultOf[inspect.Analyzer].(*inspector.Inspector)
121	
122		// Build a per-file index of lines that carry an exemption
123		// annotation, either as an inline comment on the same line or as
124		// a stand-alone comment line directly above. The annotation
125		// covers the line it sits on plus the line immediately following.
126		exempted := make(map[token.Position]bool)
127		for _, f := range pass.Files {
128			for _, cg := range f.Comments {
129				for _, c := range cg.List {
130					if !strings.Contains(c.Text, exemptionAnnotation) {
131						continue
132					}
133					if !exemptionRE.MatchString(c.Text) {
134						pass.Report(analysis.Diagnostic{
135							Pos:     c.Pos(),
136							Message: `routinglint: ` + exemptionAnnotation + ` annotation is missing a non-empty reason="..." clause`,
137						})
138						continue
139					}
140					cp := pass.Fset.Position(c.Pos())
141					exempted[token.Position{Filename: cp.Filename, Line: cp.Line}] = true
142					exempted[token.Position{Filename: cp.Filename, Line: cp.Line + 1}] = true
143				}
144			}
145		}
146	
147		isExempt := func(pos token.Pos) bool {
148			p := pass.Fset.Position(pos)
149			return exempted[token.Position{Filename: p.Filename, Line: p.Line}]
150		}
151	
152		report := func(pos token.Pos, msg string) {
153			if isExempt(pos) {
154				return
155			}
156			pass.Report(analysis.Diagnostic{Pos: pos, Message: msg})
157		}
158	
159		// Forbidden identifiers — flag at every occurrence, both
160		// declarations and references. Matching is exact on Ident.Name so
161		// substrings inside larger identifiers (e.g. the test name
162		// TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders) do not trip the lint.
163		insp.Preorder([]ast.Node{(*ast.Ident)(nil)}, func(n ast.Node) {
164			id := n.(*ast.Ident)
165			if reason, ok := forbiddenIdents[id.Name]; ok {
166				report(id.Pos(), "routinglint: forbidden identifier "+id.Name+" — "+reason)
167			}
168		})
169	
170		// Forbidden string literals — flag the literal node itself.
171		insp.Preorder([]ast.Node{(*ast.BasicLit)(nil)}, func(n ast.Node) {
172			lit := n.(*ast.BasicLit)
173			if lit.Kind != token.STRING {
174				return
175			}
176			// Trim surrounding quote/backtick so we compare raw value.
177			s := lit.Value
178			if len(s) >= 2 && (s[0] == '"' || s[0] == '`') {
179				s = s[1 : len(s)-1]
180			}
181			if reason, ok := forbiddenLiterals[s]; ok {
182				report(lit.Pos(), "routinglint: forbidden string literal "+lit.Value+" — "+reason)
183			}
184		})
185	
186		return nil, nil
187	}
